qualifications.name: BEd: Intermediate Phase (Grades 4-6)
notes: AS is calculated using Nelson Mandela University applicant-score rules: three fundamentals plus the next best subjects, Life Orientation excluded unless the official quintile bonus applies.
Source entry was marked incomplete in the supplied data.
Only the continuation of this programme is visible in the extracted table.
The source notes that Intermediate Phase students are also taught to teach Grade 7.

qualifications.name: BEd: Senior Phase and Further Education and Training (Commerce - SP Grades 7-9; FET Grades 10-12)
notes: AS is calculated using Nelson Mandela University applicant-score rules: three fundamentals plus the next best subjects, Life Orientation excluded unless the official quintile bonus applies.
To specialise in Mathematical Literacy, applicants must have taken Mathematics at school.

qualifications.name: BEd: Senior Phase and Further Education and Training (Science - SP Grades 7-9; FET Grades 10-12)
notes: AS is calculated using Nelson Mandela University applicant-score rules: three fundamentals plus the next best subjects, Life Orientation excluded unless the official quintile bonus applies.
Students choosing Physical Science must choose Mathematics as their second FET specialisation.
To specialise in Mathematical Literacy, applicants must have taken Mathematics at school.

qualifications.name: BEd: Senior Phase and Further Education and Training (Humanities - SP Grades 7-9; FET Grades 10-12)
notes: AS is calculated using Nelson Mandela University applicant-score rules: three fundamentals plus the next best subjects, Life Orientation excluded unless the official quintile bonus applies.
AS variants: Mathematics: 390 with 45%+; Technical Mathematics: 405 with 45%+; Mathematical Literacy: 405 with 60%+.
